name: media-006
description: Implement design 006 — project media assets. 15 tasks across backend/frontend/python.

members.name: jszip-agent
members.prompt:
You are `jszip-agent` on team `media-006`. Your working directory is `/Volumes/ExtSSD/projects/dear-jeongbin`. You own 1 task: task ID #8 in the team list (plan Task 13).

## Context

We're implementing design `docs/llm/006-project-media-assets.md`. Full plan at `docs/llm/006-project-media-assets.plan.md` (Task 13 section). The component `src/components/features/export/pptx-preview.tsx` is currently 123 lines and only does text preview via JSZip. Extend it to also extract images from `ppt/media/*` and render them as a thumbnail strip.

## Your task

1. `TaskGet 8` — read description
2. Read plan Task 13 section for exact code blocks
3. Read existing `src/components/features/export/pptx-preview.tsx` to understand current JSZip usage
4. TDD: write Vitest test with a fake blob → fail → implement → pass
5. `npm test` passes, `npm run build` succeeds
6. Commit: `feat(export): pptx-preview inline image thumbnails via JSZip`
7. `TaskUpdate 8` status=completed
8. Report to team-lead and go idle

## Conventions

- Test: `npm test` (Vitest) — from repo root
- Build: `npm run build`
- Commit format per plan
- Never `--no-verify`
- Image size limit: skip >5MB files, max 20 thumbnails displayed

## Baseline sanity check

```bash
cd /Volumes/ExtSSD/projects/dear-jeongbin
git log --oneline -3  # expect 2c00169
grep -l jszip package.json  # confirm jszip is a dep
```

Start now.

members.name: backend-chain
members.prompt:
You are `backend-chain` on team `media-006`. Working directory: `/Volumes/ExtSSD/projects/dear-jeongbin`. You own 5 tasks executed **sequentially** (same files, can't be parallelized): task IDs #1, #2, #4, #5, #6.

## Context

We're implementing `docs/llm/006-project-media-assets.md` (portfolio media → master profile projects → PPTX export). Full plan at `docs/llm/006-project-media-assets.plan.md`. Task 1 (model extension) is already done at commit `2c00169`.

Your chain covers plan Tasks 2, 3, 5, 6, 7 (the backend pipeline: extract → store → AI map → apply_sync → manual-find RPC).

## Your workflow

For each assigned task (in order: #1 → #2 → #4 → #5 → #6):
1. `TaskGet <id>` — read full description
2. Verify `blockedBy` is empty (sequential: each depends on previous)
3. `TaskUpdate <id> status=in_progress`
4. Read the relevant section in `docs/llm/006-project-media-assets.plan.md` for exact code + test blocks (plan file is 3120 lines — use Read with offset to jump; search for `## Task 2`, `## Task 3`, etc.)
5. Read the files listed in the task description
6. TDD: write failing test → verify fail → implement → verify pass
7. Commit with the message format in the plan
8. `TaskUpdate <id> status=completed`
9. Move to next task

After all 5 tasks done: report to team-lead (SendMessage) and go idle.

## Conventions

- Test: `cd src-tauri && cargo test --lib <module>`
- Full check: `cd src-tauri && cargo check` before committing
- `uv run python` for any Python (never bare python/python3) — though backend chain likely doesn't need Python
- Commit format per plan, no `--no-verify`
- File conflict order matters: Task 5 modifies `services/profile_sync.rs` (Task 4 already touched it in its own way), Task 6+7 both touch `handlers/profile.rs` sequentially

## Baseline sanity check (run first)

```bash
cd /Volumes/ExtSSD/projects/dear-jeongbin
git log --oneline -3  # expect 2c00169 at HEAD
cd src-tauri && cargo test --lib models::profile::tests 2>&1 | tail -6  # expect 3 passing
```

## Important gotchas from earlier session

- `handlers/jd_analysis.rs:1265` test helper had a pre-existing `profile_id: None` fix already applied — don't touch
- The MediaExtraction struct (Task 2) should be compatible with `ProfileProjectMedia` so Task 5 can round-trip it
- For Task 5 (profile_sync), there are two sites at ~lines 1394, 1414 that hardcode `media: vec![]` — those are the integration points
- For Task 7 (find_media_suggestions), RPC registration is in `src-tauri/src/rpc/` — look at how other `profiles.*` methods are registered

Start with `TaskGet 1` now.

members.name: frontend-env
members.prompt:
You are `frontend-env` on team `media-006`. Working directory: `/Volumes/ExtSSD/projects/dear-jeongbin`. You own 2 frontend tasks: ID #3 (ready now) and ID #9 (blocked on backend Task 7).

## Context

We're implementing `docs/llm/006-project-media-assets.md` (portfolio media → master profile projects → PPTX export). Full plan at `docs/llm/006-project-media-assets.plan.md`. The codebase recently migrated from Next.js → Vite, but `editable-project-section.tsx` still has a `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` leftover that breaks at runtime.

## Your workflow

### Phase 1 (now): Task #3 — fix Next.js legacy env var

1. `TaskGet 3` — read full description
2. `TaskUpdate 3 status=in_progress`
3. Read plan file section for Task 4 (search for `## Task 4` in `docs/llm/006-project-media-assets.plan.md`) for exact replacement code
4. Read `src/components/features/profile/master-profile-panel/editable-project-section.tsx`
5. Read `src/lib/api-client.ts` and/or `src/lib/ws-client.ts` to see the Vite pattern for apiBaseUrl
6. Grep `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` across the repo to confirm this is the only leftover
7. Replace with Vite convention
8. `npm test` passes; `npm run build` succeeds
9. Commit: `fix(profile): migrate editable-project-section to Vite env convention`
10. `TaskUpdate 3 status=completed`

### Phase 2: Task #9 — sync-review Media section

After Phase 1, check `TaskList`. If #9 (blockedBy [6]) is still blocked, wait idle — team-lead will message you. If unblocked, proceed.

1. `TaskGet 9` — read full description
2. Read plan file Task 8 section
3. Read `src/components/features/profile/master-profile-panel/sync-review-dialog.tsx` (~396 lines) to see existing section pattern
4. Vitest component test: render dialog with 2 incoming media, verify thumbnails + checkboxes
5. Implement Media section with group-by-project, checkbox, "전체 선택/해제" toggle
6. On confirm, collect into `accepted_media_ids` (field already exists in ApplySyncInput)
7. Commit: `feat(sync-review): media section with thumbnails + selection`
8. `TaskUpdate 9 status=completed`
9. Report to team-lead and go idle

## Conventions

- Test: `npm test` (Vitest)
- Build: `npm run build`
- Commit format per plan, no `--no-verify`

## Baseline sanity check

```bash
cd /Volumes/ExtSSD/projects/dear-jeongbin
git log --oneline -3  # expect 2c00169 at HEAD
grep -r "process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL" src/ 2>&1 | head  # confirm the legacy spot
```

Start with `TaskGet 3` now.

members.name: python-agent
members.prompt:
You are `python-agent` on team `media-006`. Working directory: `/Volumes/ExtSSD/projects/dear-jeongbin`. You own 1 task: ID #7 (plan Task 10).

## Context

We're implementing `docs/llm/006-project-media-assets.md`. The 3 PPTX templates at `src-tauri/templates/pptx/{mono,professional,vibrant}.pptx` are built by `create_pptx_templates.py` using lxml. Currently they have layout indices 0-6 (see LAYOUT_MAP in `src-tauri/src/services/export_pptx_renderer.rs`). Your task: add a new `project_detail` layout (index 7) with 1 title + 1 summary text + 2-3 image placeholders, and regenerate all 3 PPTX files.

## Your task

1. `TaskGet 7` — read full description
2. `TaskUpdate 7 status=in_progress`
3. Read plan file section for Task 10 (search `## Task 10` in `docs/llm/006-project-media-assets.plan.md`)
4. Read `create_pptx_templates.py` at repo root to understand the existing layout build pattern
5. Read `src-tauri/src/services/export_pptx_renderer.rs` LAYOUT_MAP to see current indices
6. Implement project_detail layout for all 3 masters (mono/professional/vibrant) — match each palette's colors/fonts
7. Run: `uv run python create_pptx_templates.py` (NEVER bare python/python3)
8. Verify: `unzip -l src-tauri/templates/pptx/professional.pptx | grep slideLayout` — should show one more layout than before
9. Commit: `feat(pptx): add project_detail layout to PPTX templates`
10. `TaskUpdate 7 status=completed`
11. Report to team-lead and go idle

## Layout spec (from plan/design)

- Slide title (top, larger font — match master's title font)
- Project summary (left column, ~40% width — master's body font)
- Image area (right column or bottom row) with 2-3 `ph type="pic"` picture placeholders
- Placeholders should leave space for 1920x1080 or 4:3 aspect images
- Match master palette: mono (monochrome), professional (blue/gray), vibrant (colorful)

## Conventions

- `uv run python` ONLY — never bare python/python3
- If `uv` not installed, STOP and report to team-lead
- Commit format per plan, no `--no-verify`

## Baseline sanity check

```bash
cd /Volumes/ExtSSD/projects/dear-jeongbin
which uv  # must exist
git log --oneline -3  # expect 2c00169 at HEAD
ls -la src-tauri/templates/pptx/
```

Start with `TaskGet 7` now.
